docs.title: 🎧 Introduction – Audition Toolkit
docs.text:
Audition Toolkit is an application developed by the emerging Indian company, Quantalept. The app's core purpose is to:

  • Enhance the quality of audio recorded under low-audibility conditions.
  • Improve and apply desired audio effects in pre-composed high-quality music.
  • Offer a wide range of audio processing features like EQ adjustments, pitch/tempo control, and Karaoke functionalities.

Whether you're enhancing voice recordings or refining musical compositions, Audition Toolkit puts powerful editing tools right at your fingertips.

docs.title: Features :
docs.text:
Advanced Edit - Fine-tune your audio tracks with professional controls. Adjust volume, and effects like pitch, tempo, reverb, bass, treble, and echo to create the perfect mix.

Record - Record voice, instruments, or any audio input directly within the app. Apply the same advanced effects as in Advanced Edit to instantly enhance your recordings.

Trim - Quickly cut out unwanted sections from your audio. Mark start and end points with centisecond precision for seamless edits.

Voice Changer - Transform your voice with pitch-based controls. Make it bolder, leaner, or completely different with just a simple knob. Perfect for creative projects or fun

Split - Divide your tracks into multiple segments easily. Remove or rearrange sections with a single click, keeping the workflow simple and precise.

Karaoke - Remove vocals from any track to create karaoke-ready versions. Uses AI-trained models, phase inversion, and center-channel extraction to retain music clarity while eliminating vocals.

Reverse - Play audio backwards for creative effects, musical experimentation, or to discover hidden patterns.

Magic Enhancer- Automatically enhance audio quality and clarity. Add ambience, spatial effects, and improve fidelity, giving your tracks a polished, professional sound.

Customization - General overview and personalize app settings to match your workflow.

Pro Features - Unlock premium tools for professional audio editing. Access exclusive features, advanced effects, and the full Karaoke functionality.

Common Functions - Listen to edits in real-time before saving. Ensure every change matches your expectations and fine-tune your work easily.

Data deletion - Know how to clear your usage analytic data from the audition toolkit app

Customization See the Customization section in quick_start.md for all general customization options, including changing the default settings location.

docs.title: 📖 Delete Your App Data
docs.text:
We respect your privacy and give users full control over their data. 🔐

If you would like to request deletion of your account data, recordings, analytics data, or any stored information related to the app, follow the steps below.

Once the request is processed, all associated user data will be permanently removed from our systems. ⚠️

docs.title: 📱 Steps to Request Data Deletion
docs.text:
1. Open the app settings screen.
  2. Navigate to the Privacy & Data section.
  3. Tap on Delete My Data.
  4. Confirm the deletion request.
  5. Your request will be submitted successfully. ✅

docs.title: ⚠️ Important Information
docs.text:
• Data deletion is permanent and cannot be undone.
  • Deleted recordings and exported files cannot be recovered.
  • Some temporary backup data may take a few days to fully disappear from servers.
  • Analytics data collected anonymously may remain in aggregated form without identifying users.

docs.title: 📧 Need Help?
docs.text:
If you face any issues while deleting your data, contact us through the support email provided in the app store listing.

✅ Tip: Before deleting your data, export or back up any important recordings you wish to keep.

docs.title: ✨ Description
docs.text:
Magic Enhancer is a powerful audio transformation tool that gives you full control over the listening experience using three specialized knobs:

  • Ambience Knob – lets you immerse your audience in atmospheric background sounds.
  • Fidelity Knob – sharpens your audio with crystal-clear accuracy and richness.
  • Spatial Range Knob – expands the width and depth of sound across a 3D space.

Each knob operates on a 0 to 5 scale, allowing you to finely tailor your sound to suit everything from clean studio tracks to immersive ambient compositions. With just a few adjustments, the Magic Enhancer turns raw recordings into polished, emotionally engaging soundscapes. 🎧✨

docs.title: 📖 Description
docs.text:
Ambience in audio refers to the subtle background noises that add space, presence, and realism to a recording — such as room tone, environmental sounds, and natural echoes. It’s often what gives a piece of audio its mood or sense of place.

Whether you’re enhancing a voiceover, creating cinematic atmosphere, or adding texture to music, adjusting ambience can transport listeners into the recorded scene. Think of it as the difference between a dry studio vocal and a live stage performance.

docs.title: 🎚️ Ambience Knob – User Guide
docs.text:
• Purpose: Add environmental depth by amplifying background or ambient sound.
  • Range: 0 (no ambience) to 5 (full immersion).

docs.title: Steps:
docs.text:
1. Locate the Ambience knob in the interface.
  2. Begin at 0 to retain the original, dry sound.
  3. Turn the knob slowly clockwise to add atmosphere.
  4. Suggested values:
  5. 1–2: Light background fill (good for podcasts or voiceovers).
  6. 3–4: Balanced ambient depth for immersive listening.
  7. 5: Maximum ambient richness, ideal for ambient music or cinematic audio.
  8. Preview the audio after each change and adjust according to the tone you want to create.

docs.title: 📖 Description
docs.text:
Fidelity is the measure of how accurately audio reproduces the original sound. High fidelity ("Hi-Fi") audio maintains clarity, dynamic range, and detail without introducing distortion or artificial color.

A boost in fidelity makes instruments sound crisper, vocals clearer, and subtle audio textures more noticeable. It ensures that both soft whispers and loud peaks are preserved as intended, delivering a clean and emotionally expressive listening experience.

docs.title: 🎚️ Fidelity Knob – User Guide
docs.text:
• Purpose: Increase clarity, detail, and dynamic accuracy.
  • Range: 0 (no fidelity enhancement) to 5 (maximum clarity and precision).

docs.title: Steps:
docs.text:
1. Locate the Fidelity knob in the interface.
  2. Start at 0 to hear the original signal.
  3. Turn the knob clockwise to gradually sharpen sound quality.
  4. Suggested values:
  5. 1–2: Minor improvements for voice clarity or old recordings.
  6. 3–4: Balanced clarity, great for most music and dialogue.
  7. 5: Maximum sharpness, suited for high-definition or professional-grade audio.
  8. Use your ears to check for any unnatural sharpness or over-processing.

docs.title: 📖 Description
docs.text:
Spatial range defines how sound is distributed across space — left/right, front/back, near/far. It’s what creates a wide “soundstage” that feels immersive and three-dimensional.

By enhancing spatial range, you can simulate the feel of a concert hall, a surround-sound theater, or simply give room for each instrument to breathe. It works through techniques like stereo widening, panning, and spatial processing to enhance both realism and artistic depth.

docs.title: 🎚️ Spatial Range Knob – User Guide
docs.text:
• Purpose: Expand the perceived position and movement of sound in a 3D field.
  • Range: 0 (flat stereo) to 5 (maximum surround spatial effect).

docs.title: Steps:
docs.text:
1. Locate the Spatial Range knob in the interface.
  2. Start from 0 to maintain basic stereo audio.
  3. Increase gradually to widen and deepen the sound field.
  4. Suggested values:
  5. 1–2: Slight stereo expansion for subtle depth.
  6. 3: Recommended for standard stereo speakers.
  7. 4–5: For full spatial immersion on 5.1 or 7.1 surround systems.
  8. Always preview and fine-tune to avoid artificial separation or phasing issues.

docs.title: ⚠️ Final Notes
docs.text:
• Each knob (Ambience, Fidelity, Spatial Range) functions independently and can be combined for layered effects.
  • Preview after every adjustment to match the enhancement with your playback device and content type.
  • Default recommendation: keep knobs in the 2–3 range for general listening.
  • Avoid pushing all knobs to 5 unless you're going for a dramatic or experimental sound.

docs.title: 🎛️ ADVANCED EDIT
docs.text:
Advanced Edit allows powerful post-processing of uploaded audio files. These controls help you refine the sonic character, clarity, and depth of existing tracks.

🎧 Tip: Always preview each change. Use headphones for precise sound adjustments.

docs.title: a. Bass
docs.text:
Bass enhances the low-end frequencies (20 Hz – 250 Hz), adding depth and warmth.

  • Boost sub-bass for impact (EDM, Hip-Hop).
  • Tame mid-bass to avoid muddiness.

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use:
docs.text:
1. Turn the knob clockwise to increase richness.
  2. Turn counterclockwise to reduce low-end boom.

docs.title: b. Treble
docs.text: Treble manages high-end frequencies (2 kHz – 20 kHz), adding sparkle and clarity.

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use:
docs.text:
1. Turn the knob right to brighten vocals and strings.
  2. Turn it left to smooth harsh or piercing sounds.

docs.title: c. Volume
docs.text: Volume sets the overall loudness.

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use:
docs.text:
1. Rotate right to boost.
  2. Rotate left to reduce.
  3. Find a comfortable listening level that avoids clipping.

docs.title: d. Pitch
docs.text:
Pitch adjusts how high or low the voice sounds.

  • Raise pitch = chipmunk/sharp.
  • Lower pitch = deep/bold.

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use:
docs.text:
1. Rotate knob right to raise pitch (up to 1.8x).
  2. Rotate knob left to lower pitch (down to 0.7x).

docs.title: e. Tempo
docs.text:
Tempo changes audio speed (not pitch).

  • Slower = calm, soft.
  • Faster = upbeat, energetic.

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use:
docs.text:
1. Turn left for slow-motion.
  2. Turn right to speed things up.

docs.title: f. Echo
docs.text: Echo adds reflective tail to simulate bounce and space.

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use:
docs.text:
1. Rotate right to add dreamy ambiance.
  2. Rotate left to minimize echo.

🚨 Avoid overuse—can cause muddiness.

docs.title: g. Fade
docs.text: Fade In/Out helps smooth transitions at start or end.

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use:
docs.text:
1. Tap Fade icon near waveform.
  2. Choose: Fade In, Fade Out, or Both.
  3. Drag handles to set fade length.

docs.title: h. Reverb
docs.text: Reverb simulates audio in physical spaces (like rooms or halls).

docs.title: Presets:
docs.text:
• 🏟️ Amphitheatre
  • 🏫 Classroom
  • ⛪ Cathedral
  • 🏛️ Large Church
  • 🚿 Bathroom
  • 🏰 Minster
  • 🌀 Slinky

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use:
docs.text:
1. Select preset.
  2. Use the knob to adjust the intensity. If set to 0, no changes will occur.
  3. Preview carefully—too much drowns detail.

docs.title: 📖 Karaoke Description
docs.text:
A Karaoke music extractor is a tool designed to separate vocals from the instrumental (music) track of a song. This process, known as vocal isolation or vocal removal, allows users to generate karaoke-ready versions of songs or remix audio content creatively.

While perfect vocal extraction can be challenging due to overlapping frequencies and recording complexities, modern tools use advanced machine learning models to achieve impressively accurate results. These models are trained on large datasets and apply techniques such as:

  • Center channel extraction
  • Phase inversion
  • Frequency-based filtering

Together, these methods help isolate and remove vocal elements, leaving behind a clean instrumental backing track.

docs.title: Steps:
docs.text:
1. Select the music track you want to convert into a karaoke version.
  2. Check your available Karaoke credits (displayed at the top-right corner of the screen).
  3. 1 credit is required for every 5-minute song.

  4. The credit counter may take a few seconds to update after track selection.

  5. If you have insufficient credits:

  6. Click the Credits button.
  7. You’ll be redirected to the Karaoke Upgrade page.
  8. Choose to add either 5 credits or 10 credits as per your need.

  9. Once credits are available, click the Save button to proceed.

  10. The file will now:

  11. Get compressed,
  12. Be uploaded to the server,
  13. Undergo vocal separation, and
  14. Be converted to a karaoke version.
  15. Please wait patiently — this process may take some time depending on the file size and network.
  16. Once complete, the karaoke file will be automatically downloaded to your local storage.

✅ Note: Karaoke extraction quality may vary depending on the complexity of the original mix. For best results, use high-quality audio files.

docs.title: 👁️ Feature Preview
docs.text: Each feature in the app includes a preview option to help users understand what it does before using it fully.

docs.title: 🔍 How It Works
docs.text:
• An eye-shaped icon ! appears next to each feature.
  • Clicking the icon triggers a 10-second preview by default.
  • This preview provides a quick demo of the feature without applying any permanent changes.
  • While the preview is playing, you can pause or stop it anytime using the on-screen controls.

docs.title: ⏸️ Pause & Stop Options
docs.text:
• Once a preview begins, pause and stop buttons will appear.
  • You can:
  • Tap Pause ⏸️ to temporarily halt the preview.
  • Tap Stop ⏹️ to immediately cancel the preview playback.
  • After stopping or letting the preview complete, the feature returns to its idle state.

docs.title: ⚙️ Customizing Preview Duration
docs.text:
• You can change the preview duration from the Settings page.
  • Supported range: 5 to 30 seconds.
  • To adjust the duration:
  • Go to Settings.
  • Locate the "Preview Duration" option.
  • Use the slider or input box to choose your preferred time.
  • The new setting is saved automatically and applies to all future previews.

docs.title: ✂️ Cut Feature
docs.text: Each feature in the app also includes a cut option that allows users to trim and remove unwanted portions easily.

docs.title: 🔧 How It Works
docs.text:
• A scissor-shaped icon ! is available next to every feature that supports cutting.
  • You can move the sliders to select the start and end points of the portion you want to remove.
  • Once the unwanted range is selected, just tap the scissor icon to perform the cut.
  • The selected segment will be discarded immediately, keeping only the desired parts.

docs.title: 🎯 Steps to Cut a Segment
docs.text:
1. Click on the ✂️ scissor icon next to the feature.
  2. Use the start and end sliders to mark the range to be removed.
  3. Review the selected section.
  4. Click the Cut button/icon to confirm.
  5. The unwanted portion is deleted from the content, and the remaining part is retained.

docs.title: 🧪 Example Flow
docs.text:
Feature Action Result 🎵 Advanced Edit Select 00:10 to 00:25 Removes that segment from the track 🎤 Record Trim 00:00 to 00:04 Discards leading silence

ℹ️ Note: Once cut, the removed part cannot be recovered, unless you undo (if supported).

docs.title: 💾 Save Feature
docs.text: All features in the app come with a save option that allows users to apply adjustments, rename files, and store the final output.

docs.title: 📌 How It Works
docs.text:
• A save icon ! is shown at the top-right corner of each feature.
  • Clicking this icon:
  • Applies all adjustments made (via knobs, sliders, etc.).
  • Prompts the user to rename the file and select the desired output format (e.g., .mp3, .wav).
  • Once the details are filled, tap the Save button at the bottom to complete the process.

docs.title: 🗂️ File Storage
docs.text:
• Saved audio files (music tracks or voice recordings) are stored inside the Audition folder.
  • Each save creates a new version with the updated settings and user-defined name.

docs.title: 🎛️ Split Segment Saving
docs.text:
• If your content is split into multiple segments (e.g., via the Cut feature), each segment is saved as a separate file.
  • The saved files follow a structured naming pattern like:
  • Track_Segment_1.mp3
  • Track_Segment_2.mp3

  • ...

  • After saving, the first segment plays automatically.

  • Tap the Next button to play the next saved segment in order.

docs.title: Why Go Pro?
docs.text: To unlock powerful features like Karaoke, advanced audio editing tools, and the Voice Changer, you’ll need to upgrade to Pro — a one-time or yearly paid plan designed for users who want the full audio experience.

docs.title: 💰 How to Upgrade:
docs.text:
1. On the home screen, tap the money icon located at the top right corner.
  2. This will take you to the "Upgrade to Pro" page.
  3. You'll see a list of exclusive features unlocked by the Pro version.
  4. At the bottom, you’ll find two purchase options:
  5. 1 Year Pro – ₹50
  6. Lifetime Pro – ₹200
  7. Select your preferred plan.
  8. Proceed with the Google Play payment popup that appears.
  9. Once the payment is successful, the Pro features will be automatically unlocked.

docs.title: 🔁 Restore Purchase (Cross-Device Access)
docs.text:
If you've already purchased Pro and want to access it on another device:

  • Just tap the "Restore Purchase" button on the Upgrade to Pro screen.
  • Make sure you're logged in with the same Google account used for the original purchase.
  • This will instantly unlock Pro features on the new device — no need to pay again.

✅ Tip: Pro unlocks smooth, ad-free, and feature-rich audio editing. Perfect for creators who want full control and quality output.

docs.title: 📁 Default File Save Location
docs.text:
Every file you edit or export will be automatically saved to a folder named Audition inside your device’s Music folder.

❗ This design is intentional: newer Android versions request folder access every time if you try to save somewhere else. To keep it smooth, we use the Music/Audition folder by default — no repeated permission prompts!

docs.title: ⚙️ Settings Customization
docs.text:
From the bottom-right Settings icon, you can modify several important preferences:

  • Default Format Change your audio export format between .mp3, .wav, or .aac.

  • Preview Duration Choose how long the preview should be: 10s, 20s, or 30s — helpful for quickly reviewing longer tracks.

  • Length Filter Enable this to automatically skip short samples such as alert tones, notifications, or ringtones — perfect for keeping your workspace clean.

docs.title: 🕘 Recent Files
docs.text: In the bottom pane, you’ll find a tab called Recents. It displays your recently edited audio files so you can easily reopen and modify them later without searching manually.

docs.title: 👁️ Pro Tip:
docs.text: Always preview your audio using the 👁️ Preview icon before exporting! It ensures your edits are just right.

docs.title: 📬 Need Help?
docs.text:
For queries, suggestions, or feedback, feel free to reach out at 📧 atsupport@quantalept.com

Let the learning and creativity begin!

docs.title: 🎙️ RECORD
docs.text:
The Record feature allows you to capture audio live with real-time control over effects like pitch, bass, echo, reverb, and more. It’s designed to let you shape your voice or sound from the very beginning—perfect for podcasters, voice artists, and creators.

🎤 Pro Tip: Record in a quiet environment with a good microphone to ensure clean and crisp audio.

docs.title: 🎚️ Real-Time Controls While Recording
docs.text:
You can adjust these live during recording: - Bass - Treble - Pitch - Reverb - Echo - Volume - Fade - Tempo

These controls work just like in Advanced Edit, but in real-time.

docs.title: 🔧 How to Use Record:
docs.text:
1. Tap the "Record" button on the main screen.

  2. A recording panel opens—click Start Recording 🟥 to begin.

  3. During recording:

  4. Use Pause Recording ⏸️ to temporarily stop.
  5. Tap Resume Recording ▶️ to continue from where you left off.
  6. During recording, you can adjust the sensitivity in decibels to reduce background noise and prevent the capture of unwanted sounds.

  7. Once you're done, tap Stop ⏹️ to finish recording.

  8. After stopping, you’ll be taken to the Advanced Edit screen where you can:

  9. Apply all pro-level effects (bass, pitch, tempo, etc.).
  10. Tweak your recorded audio the same way you would edit an uploaded file.
  11. Once editing is complete, tap the Save button (📥 on the top-right corner).
  12. Choose your desired file name and audio format, then tap Save to export your final audio file.

🎧 This seamless workflow lets you: - Capture → Preview → Edit → Save All in one smooth and powerful flow.

✅ Tip: You can pause and resume as many times as needed to create multi-part recordings in a single file.

docs.title: 🔄 REVERSAL
docs.text:
The reverse effect is an audio transformation where the selected portion of the sound is played backwards, creating an unusual and often surreal effect. This process inverts the waveform so that it plays in reverse order, turning even familiar sounds into something eerie, dreamy, or otherworldly.

🎧 Use cases: - Add suspense or mystery in background scores - Make transitions more creative in remixes - Reveal Easter eggs or hidden phrases (a trick often used in old-school music production!)

Reversing a vocal line, instrument stroke, or ambient sound can surprise the listener, often catching attention with its haunting or psychedelic tone.

docs.title: 🛠️ User Guide:
docs.text:
1. Select the audio file from your library.
  2. Trim the section you want to reverse using the start and end markers (if you want only a portion reversed).
  3. Click Save and enter a title (optional).
  4. Your reversed audio will be processed and saved automatically.

📝 Note: Only the selected trimmed portion will be reversed—not the entire file—unless you choose the whole duration.

docs.title: 📖 Split Description
docs.text:
Split refers to the process of dividing an audio track into multiple segments. This is especially useful when you want to isolate specific parts of a track—like extracting a chorus, removing a section, or preparing samples for remixes.

Splitting is a fundamental editing technique widely used in music production, podcast editing, interviews, and content creation to extract only the needed portions and enhance clarity. Whether you’re fine-tuning a single phrase or breaking down an entire song into parts, the split tool gives you precise control over audio segmentation.

docs.title: Steps:
docs.text:
1. Select the audio file you want to edit.
  2. Once loaded, the audio waveform appears along with a split marker at the beginning.
  3. Drag the split marker to the point where you want to divide the track.
  4. Click the Split icon (located near the Preview button on the play bar). → This creates two segments: one before the marker and one after.
  5. You can repeat this process to create multiple segments by moving the marker to different positions and splitting again.
  6. Each segment can be renamed, reordered, or deleted. ⚠️ Deleting a segment moves it to another section for recovery. 🔪 Use the Cut option if you want to permanently discard it.
  7. Once satisfied, click Save on the top right corner.
  8. Choose your output format (e.g., MP3, WAV).
  9. Optionally, enter a new title for your split file.
  10. Finally, hit the Save button at the bottom to export your edited audio.

✅ Tip: Preview the audio after each split to ensure that transitions are clean and no valuable content is missed.

docs.title: 📖 Trim Description
docs.text:
Trim refers to the process of cutting out unwanted segments from the beginning, end, or even middle of an audio track. It’s a fundamental audio editing technique used to eliminate silence, errors, noise, or irrelevant sections.

Trimming ensures a clean, focused, and professional-sounding result. It’s commonly used in music production, podcasts, voiceovers, interviews, and all audio-based content to enhance clarity and flow.

docs.title: Steps:
docs.text:
1. Select the audio file you want to edit.
  2. Once loaded, the waveform of the audio will appear with trim handles (markers) at both ends.
  3. Drag the handles to highlight the part of the audio you want to keep.
  4. If you want to remove a specific middle section, select that region and click the cut icon below the waveform — this will delete the selected part.
  5. For precision editing, use the Start and End time fields to enter exact trimming values.
  6. Click the Save button at the top right of the screen once you're satisfied with your trim.
  7. Choose your output format (e.g., MP3, WAV).
  8. Optionally, enter a new title for the edited file.
  9. Finally, click the Save button at the bottom to export your trimmed audio to local storage.

✅ Tip: Always preview your selection before cutting or saving to avoid accidentally removing important audio.

docs.title: 📖 Voice Changer Description
docs.text:
A Voice Changer is a powerful audio effect that allows you to modify the pitch of a voice, making it sound bolder or leaner by adjusting its frequency. 🎶

  • Lowering the pitch results in a deeper, bolder voice — ideal for dramatic, intense, or authoritative tones.
  • Raising the pitch creates a lighter, leaner voice — perfect for playful, cartoonish, or child-like effects.

This effect works by modifying the frequency and timing of the voice waveform. Whether you're adding character, emotion, or fun to your audio, the Voice Changer makes it easy to transform voices creatively. 🔄🔊

docs.title: Steps:
docs.text:
1. Import or open the audio file you want to edit.
  2. Use the single knob on the screen to adjust pitch:
  3. Turn counterclockwise to lower the pitch and create a bolder, deeper voice (minimum: 0.7).
  4. Turn clockwise to raise the pitch and produce a leaner, lighter voice (maximum: 1.8).
  5. Apply small, gradual changes to avoid distorted or unnatural results.
  6. Preview the audio after each adjustment to fine-tune the pitch to your liking.
  7. Once you're happy with the sound:
  8. Click the Save button at the top right of the screen.
  9. Choose your output format (e.g., MP3, WAV) and rename the file if needed.
  10. Click the Save button at the bottom to export your edited audio.

✅ Tip: Lower pitch works great for villain voices or narration. Higher pitch adds fun and comedy to your audio — try both and see what fits your style!
